L1.area: Transparency log entry type processing and validation logic

L2.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists where a proposed log entry with attacker-controlled missing or empty fields can cause an uninitialized internal structure to be dereferenced during entry canonicalization, leading to a panic that crashes the processing thread.

L3.area: COSE and DSSE signing envelope entry type processing and canonicalization
L3.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists where a proposed COSE signing entry with an empty message field passes validation but leaves an internal signing message structure uninitialized, which is later dereferenced during entry canonicalization, causing a panic. A similar nil dereference also affects DSSE envelope entry processing where missing envelope or signature fields are not checked before use.

ground_truth.reason: The COSE v0.0.1 entry type's validate function returns success when the message field is empty, leaving the sign1Msg struct uninitialized; Canonicalize then dereferences sign1Msg.Payload, causing a nil pointer panic. Similar nil pointer dereferences exist in the DSSE v0.0.1 entry type.
